description: Packaging, cooking, build errors, platform deployment, and shipping issues in UE5

entries.title: Packaging Fails with Cook Errors
entries.description: Project packaging fails during the cook stage with asset errors, missing references, or unknown cook failures.
entries.rootCause: Cook errors typically come from: missing asset references (soft references to deleted assets), circular dependencies, assets with invalid properties, or Blueprint compilation errors that only surface during cooking.
solution: Read the full Output Log - cook errors include the specific asset path that failed
Fix or redirect missing references using 'Asset Audit' (right-click asset > Audit)
Run 'Validate Assets' from the content browser to find issues before cooking
Delete Saved/Cooked and Intermediate folders, then retry
Enable 'Cook on the Fly' in editor settings for faster iteration
Check that all referenced plugins are enabled for the target platform
For map-specific errors, try cooking only the main game map first

entries.title: Shader Compilation Errors During Build
entries.description: Build or packaging fails because materials have shader compilation errors that don't appear in the editor.
entries.rootCause: The editor compiles shaders for your current platform/RHI only. Packaging compiles for the target platform which may have different shader model requirements. Materials using features unsupported on the target platform fail.
solution: Check which target platform the shader fails on - the error log includes the shader platform
Ensure materials are compatible with the target shader model (e.g., mobile uses ES3.1)
Remove unsupported nodes from materials targeting mobile (no tessellation, limited texture samples)
Preview target platform shaders in editor: Change Preview Platform in toolbar
Clear derived data cache: delete DerivedDataCache folder and rebuild
For custom shaders, verify HLSL syntax matches the target platform compiler

entries.title: C++ Build Error: Module Not Found / Missing Include
entries.description: C++ compilation fails because a header file or module can't be found.
entries.rootCause: The module that contains the header is not listed in your .Build.cs dependencies. UE5 enforces strict module boundaries - you can't include headers from modules you don't depend on.
solution: Add the required module to your .Build.cs file in PublicDependencyModuleNames or PrivateDependencyModuleNames
Use Public for modules whose types appear in your public headers
Use Private for modules only used in your .cpp files
Common modules to add: 'UMG' (widgets), 'AIModule' (AI), 'NavigationSystem' (nav), 'Niagara' (particles), 'EnhancedInput' (input)
For engine headers, check the UE docs to find which module provides the class
After updating Build.cs, regenerate project files (right-click .uproject > Generate Project Files)

entries.title: Packaging Succeeds but Game Crashes on Launch
entries.description: The project packages successfully but the built game crashes on launch or shortly after.
entries.rootCause: Common causes: assets loaded via string path (LoadObject with hardcoded path) that aren't included in the pak file, Blueprint-only references that the cooker can't discover, check/ensure assertions that fire only in shipping builds, or missing config settings.
solution: Run the packaged build from command line to see the crash log: YourGame.exe -log
Check Saved/Logs/YourProject.log in the packaged build folder
Add assets to the 'Additional Asset Directories to Cook' in Project Settings > Packaging
Replace hardcoded LoadObject paths with UPROPERTY soft references
Test in 'Standalone Game' mode (PIE dropdown) before packaging - it's closer to packaged behavior
For shipping builds, wrap check() calls that should survive: use ensure() instead
Verify DefaultGame.ini has correct map/gamemode settings for the packaged build

entries.title: Build Time Extremely Slow
entries.description: C++ compilation takes too long, either for full rebuilds or incremental builds.
entries.rootCause: Header changes trigger recompilation of all files that include that header. Including heavy engine headers in your headers (instead of forward-declaring) causes massive rebuild chains. Unity builds (combining .cpp files) help full builds but can slow incremental.
solution: Forward-declare classes in headers instead of #including their headers
Move #includes to .cpp files whenever possible
Use the 'Include What You Use' (IWYU) model that UE5 encourages
For faster iteration, use Live Coding (Ctrl+Alt+F11) instead of full rebuilds
Enable Unreal Build Accelerator (UBA) for distributed builds if available
Set BuildConfiguration to use more parallel processes: -MaxParallelActions flag
Use an SSD for your project and Intermediate folders
Precompiled headers are automatic in UE - don't disable them

entries.title: Android / iOS Build Fails
entries.description: Packaging for mobile platforms (Android/iOS) fails with SDK, gradle, or Xcode errors.
entries.rootCause: Mobile builds require platform SDKs (Android SDK/NDK, Xcode) to be properly installed and configured. Version mismatches between UE5 and the SDK, missing provisioning profiles (iOS), or incorrect gradle settings (Android) cause failures.
solution: For Android: use the 'SetupAndroid' batch script in Engine/Extras/Android to configure SDK
Verify SDK paths in Project Settings > Platforms > Android/iOS
Check UE5 release notes for required SDK/NDK versions for your engine version
For iOS: ensure a valid provisioning profile and signing certificate are configured in Xcode
For Android: check gradle version compatibility - UE5 bundles its own gradle
Delete Intermediate and Build folders for the platform, then retry
Build for Development config first (not Shipping) to get better error messages

entries.title: Plugin Build Errors / Plugin Not Loading
entries.description: A plugin fails to compile or the editor says the plugin module could not be loaded.
entries.rootCause: The plugin was built for a different engine version, a required dependency module is missing, the plugin's .uplugin descriptor has errors, or binary-only plugins don't match the current build configuration.
solution: Rebuild the plugin for your exact UE5 version - minor version differences can break binary compatibility
Check the Output Log for the specific module load error
Verify the .uplugin file has correct Module definitions and dependencies
For Marketplace plugins: re-download for your engine version from the Launcher
For source plugins: ensure all dependencies are listed in the plugin's .Build.cs
Try deleting Binaries and Intermediate folders inside the plugin directory and rebuilding
Check that the plugin's target platform matches your build target

entries.title: Content Missing in Packaged Build
entries.description: Assets, maps, or data that work in the editor are missing in the packaged build.
entries.rootCause: The cooker only includes assets that are referenced by the maps listed for cooking. Assets loaded dynamically (by string path, from data tables, or from runtime logic) are not automatically discovered and included.
solution: Add maps to 'List of maps to include' in Project Settings > Packaging
Add asset directories to 'Additional Asset Directories to Cook' in Packaging settings
Use 'Additional Asset Directories to Package' for non-cooked files (configs, data)
Replace LoadObject string paths with UPROPERTY references or Primary Asset IDs
Use Asset Manager with Primary Asset Labels to explicitly include asset groups
For DataTables, ensure the DataTable asset itself is referenced from a cooked map or included explicitly

entries.title: Dedicated Server Build Issues
entries.description: Building a dedicated server target fails, or the server build crashes at runtime.
entries.rootCause: The project doesn't have a Server .Target.cs file, or code uses client-only features (rendering, audio, widgets) in server code paths. The dedicated server has no renderer - any rendering code crashes.
solution: Create a YourProjectServer.Target.cs file with Type = TargetType.Server
Guard client-only code with #if !UE_SERVER or !WITH_SERVER_CODE
Never access UMG widgets, materials, or audio on the server
Check that GameMode is set correctly for dedicated server (GameMode runs on server only)
Use IsRunningDedicatedServer() for runtime checks
Ensure server-required plugins are enabled for the Server target
